data.title: Project Costing Gaps Leave IT Leaders Unable to Prove Value or Defend Spend, Finds Info-Tech Research Group
data.description: Increasing expenses and changing market dynamics are compelling IT executives to provide more accurate justifications for technology expenditures. Despite this, numerous companies continue to use inadequate models that fail to consider total cost of ownership, risk factors, and the lasting benefits of investments. Recent findings from Info-Tech reveal that organizations must adopt more comprehensive approaches to evaluating technology investments, considering all these factors to make informed decisions.

data.title: Cresco Labs Awarded Texas Medical Cannabis License
data.description: Cresco Labs Inc., a prominent player in the cannabis industry with a portfolio of leading brands and Sunnyside dispensaries, has been provisionally granted a Texas Compassionate Use Program License. This vertically integrated license permits Cresco Labs (CSE: CL) (OTCQX: CRLBF) (FSE: 6CQ) to engage in the cultivation, processing, and distribution of medical cannabis in Texas. The Company's achievement of this license will give Texas patients additional access to medical cannabis products.

data.title: Vericel Announces BARDA Award Valued at up to $197 Million for Procurement and Advanced Development of NexoBrid
data.description: Vericel Corporation, a leading provider of advanced therapies for sports medicine and severe burn care, has secured a lucrative ten-year contract from the U.S. Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA), a division of the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR) under the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS). This contract, totaling up to $197 million, includes various components such as the procurement of NexoBrid®, implementation and upkeep of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) system, creation and validation of a manufacturing facility in the U.S., and the enhancement of NexoBrid with a next-generation formulation and new indication.

data.title: EXL announces a strategic services collaboration with Google Cloud
data.description: The partnership between EXL and Google Cloud highlights EXL's expertise as a leading provider of AI and cloud transformation solutions. This collaboration emphasizes EXL's position as a unique partner focused on delivering tangible outcomes.

data.title: Kura Sushi Likely To Report Wider Q2 Loss; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Kura Sushi USA, Inc. (NASDAQ: KRUS) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings on April 7. Analysts are projecting a quarterly loss of 20 cents per share, compared to a loss of 14 cents per share in the same period last year. The expected quarterly revenue for the Irvine, California-based company is $77.59 million, up from $64.89 million reported last year. Claudia Schaefer was recently elected to Kura Sushi's board of directors. The company's stock price dropped by 2.4% to $67.71. For more details and analyst ratings, interested readers can visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Ray 2.55 update introduces fault tolerance for large-scale AI models, reducing downtime risk for distributed inference systems.
data.description: An update to Anyscale's Ray Serve has enabled DP group fault tolerance for vLLM WideEP deployments, reducing downtime risk for distributed AI inference systems. This update is crucial for maintaining system stability and ensuring uninterrupted AI processing across a network. The new feature enhances the reliability of the deployment by incorporating fault tolerance measures for DP groups, significantly reducing the risk of downtime. This advancement is particularly significant for distributed AI systems, where even minor interruptions can have a considerable impact. Overall, the update aims to improve the efficiency and dependability of Anyscale's Ray Serve system, benefiting organizations relying on distributed AI for their operations.

data.title: b1BANK Names Ben Marmande Director of Corporate Banking
data.description: In Baton Rouge, Louisiana, on April 2nd, 2026, b1BANK disclosed that Ben Marmande is now the director of corporate banking. With this appointment, Marmande will oversee b1BANK's corporate banking responsibilities for Texas, focusing on increasing client base, enhancing relationships, and advancing the bank’s commercial operations.

data.title: The University of Colorado Anschutz Gates Institute Achieves First-in-U.S. FDA Clearance for Campus-Built CAR T-Cell Therapy
data.description: The University of Colorado Anschutz Gates Institute has attained Investigational New Drug clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for a novel treatment aimed at adults with difficult-to-treat acute myeloid leukemia. This marks a significant milestone as the treatment moves into its first clinical trial. The clearance was received on April 2, 2026. The university is now poised to test this groundbreaking approach on a group of patients suffering from this challenging form of leukemia. This advancement brings hope to individuals battling acute myeloid leukemia, with the potential to make a positive impact on the lives of many.

data.title: RLDatix's Connected Healthcare Summit Draws 400+ Health System Leaders as Company Advances AI-Powered Patient Safety and Provider Performance Solutions
data.description: RLDatix, a prominent global provider of safety, workforce, and data solutions, unveiled groundbreaking AI-powered features alongside showcasing real-world case studies from top health systems to tackle key issues in healthcare. The announcement was made on April 2, 2026, in Nashville, Tennessee. With a track record of innovation, RLDatix continues to lead the way in addressing healthcare challenges.

data.title: Shrub Oak International School Highlights Founders Pillar Curriculum: A Comprehensive Five-Pillar Approach for Autistic Students with Complex Needs
data.description: In Mohegan Lake, N.Y., on April 2, 2026, the Shrub Oak International School announced the launch of its Founders Program, which is based on the Founders Pillar Curriculum. This unique educational model is focused on a five-pillar framework developed by Shrub Oak to cater to the needs of individuals with autism. The program aims to provide specialized support and education to students, with a particular emphasis on tailored learning and development strategies.

data.title: New AI models match top performance at 90% less cost; GLM-5 and MiniMax M2.7 now compete with Claude and GPT, reducing expenses from $250/day to $12/day for high-volume tasks. Explore more.
data.description: Recent LangChain benchmark reports indicate that GLM-5 and MiniMax M2.7 technologies are now competing strongly with Claude and GPT in agent task performance. These advancements have led to a significant reduction in costs for high-volume applications, dropping from $250 per day to just $12 per day. This shift in capabilities and affordability is a notable development in the industry.

data.title: Engine and Nuqleous Complete Strategic Merger: Creating an End-to-End Omnichannel Retail Intelligence Platform for CPGs
data.description: A newly merged company now serves over 200 customers by offering a cohesive platform that covers data integration, analytics, AI-driven insights, and planogram automation. Based in ROGERS, Ark. as of April 2, 2026, the combined entity consists of Engine, a CPG analytics and data science provider, and Nuqleous®, specializing in space planning.

data.title: Integro Bank and Global Chamber Announce April CEO Club Event Featuring Paco Underhill: "A Powerful Leadership Truth: To Compete Globally, We Must Think Locally"
data.description: On April 9, 2026, Integro Bank and the Global Chamber are joining forces to present the April CEO Club Event in Phoenix. The event will showcase Paco Underhill, a renowned retail expert and author, and will delve into practical leadership strategies. This collaboration aims to offer valuable insights to attendees and foster networking opportunities.
